api: Cin7 Core (DEAR Inventory) External API v2 (https://inventory.dearsystems.com/externalapi/v2)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, researched directly against Cin7 Core's published Apiary API Blueprint (the help-center HTML pages 403/JS-render and are not independently fetchable, but the underlying Apiary blueprint markdown source is). Covered: the 5 legacy-parity list streams (full-refresh, matching legacy), 10 additional list/reference streams (product families, product availability, and 8 reference/lookup tables), and 13 write actions across 6 resource groups (products, customers, suppliers, product categories, brands, payment terms). Excluded, by category: (1) `out_of_scope` for the large workflow-state-machine resource groups (Sale, Purchase [deprecated in favor of Advanced Purchase], Advanced Purchase, Finished Goods, Disassembly, Production, Stock [adjustments/takes/transfers], Money Task, Journal, CRM) — each carries deeply nested Order/Invoice/Payment/Fulfilment/BOM sub-objects representing a multi-step business workflow, not a flat CRUD resource, and is a genuine Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage; SaleList/PurchaseList already give full summary-level read coverage of the same underlying data. (2) `requires_elevated_scope` for Bank Accounts (financial account balances/reconciliation), Fixed Asset Type (depreciation-schedule accounting configuration), and Chart-of-Accounts writes (accounting-integration-reserved account roles) — all finance-module configuration this connector's write scope was never intended to reach. (3) `non_data_endpoint` for Me (tenant-level company settings), Webhooks (delivery-subscription management), and Templates (document layout configuration) — none are business data. (4) `duplicate_of` for Reference Books sub-resources that mirror data already covered by a primary stream. Every exclusion below is a REAL, researched category+reason, not a blanket bucket.

excluded.reason: irreversibly deletes a product-catalog entry along with its stock history/BOM/supplier links; not modeled as a write action this pass (breadth-vs-cost triage on the riskiest product mutation)
irreversibly deletes a customer record and its sale history linkage; not modeled as a write action this pass
irreversibly deletes a supplier record and its purchase history linkage; not modeled as a write action this pass
irreversibly deletes a brand referenced by existing products; not modeled as a write action this pass (breadth-vs-cost triage)
irreversibly deletes a payment term referenced by existing customers/suppliers; breadth-vs-cost triage
irreversibly deletes a warehouse location referenced by existing stock/products
irreversibly deletes a shipping carrier referenced by existing sales
irreversibly deletes a Chart of Accounts entry that may be referenced by existing products/customers/suppliers/transactions
irreversibly deletes a unit of measure referenced by existing products
Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage: a simple single-field reference write, but carrier CRUD was not prioritized over the 6 resource groups this pass already covers create+update for
same triage as POST /ref/carrier

creates a live Chart of Accounts entry that (per Cin7's own docs) is disabled outright while a Xero/QuickBooks accounting integration is enabled, and can carry SystemAccount/SystemAccountCode linkage into accounting-system-reserved account roles; accounting-system configuration is out of this connector's intended write scope
same accounting-integration-conflict concern as POST /ref/account
Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage: simple single-field reference write not prioritized this pass
same triage as POST /ref/unit
Tax rule CRUD writes touch live sale/purchase tax-calculation configuration (TaxPercent/Account linkage) shared by every future transaction; read-only this pass

the Sale resource group is a deeply nested workflow state machine (Order -> Fulfilment[Pick/Pack/Ship] -> Invoice -> CreditNote -> Payments), each sub-endpoint mutating one stage of a live sales-order lifecycle with side effects on inventory allocation and accounting postings; saleList already gives full summary-level read parity with legacy, and modeling the full workflow is a large, genuinely separate scope (Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, consistent with SaleList/PurchaseList being the pre-existing legacy-parity read surface)

Cin7 Core's own Apiary docs mark GET /purchase itself deprecated ("Please use Advanced Purchase endpoint"); the entire Purchase workflow sub-resource tree inherits the same deprecation, and PurchaseList already gives full summary-level read parity with legacy

the current (non-deprecated) replacement for the Purchase workflow tree — same nested-workflow-state-machine shape and breadth-vs-cost reasoning as the Sale group above; PurchaseList already covers summary-level read parity
binary file attachment management (upload/list/delete arbitrary files against a product/family record) is a document-management sub-feature, not row-shaped business data

manufacturing/production-order, disassembly, finished-goods-build, write-off, stocktake, stock-transfer, and manual-money/journal-posting workflows are each their own deeply nested operational sub-system (production BOMs, factory calendars, work centers, run scheduling) with real accounting/inventory side effects; a large, genuinely separate scope from this connector's core catalog/customer/supplier/order-summary surface — Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
Cin7 Core's bundled CRM module (leads/opportunities/tasks/workflow automation) is a separate business domain from inventory/order management; out of scope for this connector
bank account balance/reconciliation data and linkage is finance-module configuration this connector's scope was never intended to reach

fixed-asset depreciation-schedule configuration (depreciation method/rate/effective-life, linked to Chart of Accounts asset/depreciation account codes) is accounting-module configuration out of this connector's intended scope

custom product attribute-set SCHEMA definitions (up to 10 named/typed custom fields) are a catalog-configuration meta-resource, not row-shaped business data; products.AdditionalAttribute1-10 (the VALUES) already flow through on the products stream wherever the raw Product object carries them
product markup-price-list and bill-of-materials CONFIGURATION are catalog-setup sub-resources of Product/ProductFamily, not independently requested this pass

Reference Books sub-resources that are narrower, request-scoped views over data already reachable through a primary stream — /product-suppliers duplicates the raw Product object's own Suppliers[] sub-array, and per-customer custom prices / discount rules / ship-zone rate tables are pricing/shipping CONFIGURATION sub-resources of Product/Sale, not independently requested this pass
tenant-level company settings (company name, base currency, timezone, financial lock date) — connection/environment metadata, not a business-data resource
the connected tenant's OWN company addresses/contacts, not a customer/supplier-facing business-data resource
webhook-subscription management (registering delivery URLs) is integration plumbing, not itself a business-data resource
document/email template configuration (invoice, PO, quote layouts), not business data
a cross-object activity-feed/audit-log style endpoint spanning every transaction type; a genuinely separate reporting surface from this connector's per-object streams, not prioritized this pass
customer-scoped sub-resources (default line-item templates, store credit balances) narrower than and derivable from the customers stream's own record; not independently requested this pass
supplier-scoped prepayment/deposit sub-resource narrower than and derivable from the suppliers stream's own record; not independently requested this pass
